jokes.text: Hey, look, Uncle Phil, no disrespect, man... but you got to watch where you parking that thing.
jokes.setup: Will has apparently just bumped into Uncle Phil
jokes.punchline: Treating Uncle Phil's body like a parked vehicle that needs warning signals
jokes.explanation: Classic Will roast of Uncle Phil's weight with clever vehicle metaphor. Well-structured with fake politeness followed by the burn.

jokes.text: Man, you know, get some flashing lights or some horns or something.
jokes.setup: Continuing the 'Uncle Phil as vehicle' metaphor
jokes.punchline: Suggesting Uncle Phil needs actual traffic safety equipment
jokes.explanation: Solid escalation of the vehicle joke, though less surprising than the initial setup.

jokes.text: I'm gonna just go ahead and shut up now.
jokes.setup: Uncle Phil's presumably intimidating reaction to being called a vehicle
jokes.punchline: Will's immediate tactical retreat
jokes.explanation: Perfect comedic beat capping the sequence. The economy and timing make it work.

jokes.text: Thanks a lot for sending me to Young Republicans Camp. Yosemite's really neat. Hey, I'm rooming with Newt's nephew, Fig.
jokes.setup: Carlton at summer camp
jokes.punchline: It's specifically Young Republicans Camp with political connections
jokes.explanation: Perfectly Carlton - the earnest tone about something ridiculous. 'Fig' as Newt's nephew is a great absurd detail.

jokes.text: I wouldn't make fun of them. These kids are the future leaders of this country.
jokes.setup: Will presumably mocking Young Republicans
jokes.punchline: Uncle Phil's deadpan defense
jokes.explanation: Solid straight-man response that plays into the political divide between Will and Phil.

jokes.text: Please, me miss Carlton? Pfft. I forgot all about him. [beat] Did he say when he coming back?
jokes.setup: Will denying he misses Carlton
jokes.punchline: Immediately asking when Carlton returns
jokes.explanation: Classic contradiction joke with perfect timing. The immediate reversal is what sells it.

jokes.text: Did he say when he coming back?
jokes.setup: Immediately after claiming he forgot about Carlton
jokes.punchline: Will immediately asks when Carlton's returning, proving he misses him
jokes.explanation: Perfect comedic contradiction - the quick turnaround makes Will's denial obviously false

jokes.text: What say we go hook something up now? I'll help you practice.
jokes.setup: Hilary announcing she's cooking with Wolfgang Puck
jokes.punchline: Will volunteering to help cook - showing his desperation
jokes.explanation: Character moment showing Will's boredom, though the joke itself isn't exceptional.

jokes.text: Help me cook? You must really be bored.
jokes.setup: Will offering to help cook
jokes.punchline: Hilary immediately recognizing this as desperate behavior
jokes.explanation: Good character beat - Hilary being unexpectedly perceptive about Will's state.

jokes.text: I mean, it's bad enough that she gets hookers for married men... but blue eye shadow? Hello.
jokes.setup: Discussing the Show-Biz Madam's crimes
jokes.punchline: Hilary being more offended by the fashion choice than prostitution
jokes.explanation: Quintessential Hilary - completely missing the moral point for a superficial one. Perfect character integration and misdirection.

jokes.text: Yeah, you know, that is a little stank.
jokes.setup: Hilary's blue eye shadow comment
jokes.punchline: Will agreeing with the wrong priority
jokes.explanation: Decent tag to Hilary's joke but doesn't add much beyond agreement.

jokes.text: Imagine that.
jokes.setup: Will saying imagine a dude so desperate he'd pay for sex
jokes.punchline: Geoffrey's knowing, deadpan response
jokes.explanation: Perfect Geoffrey deadpan delivery. The economy is flawless - two words that imply so much.

jokes.text: It's a couple of these other rabble-rousers you need to be worried about.
jokes.setup: Uncle Phil asking Will to behave
jokes.punchline: Will deflecting responsibility to unnamed others
jokes.explanation: Standard deflection joke - functional but not exceptional.

jokes.text: Oh, dear God.
jokes.setup: Hilary wanting to watch Geoffrey cook
jokes.punchline: Geoffrey's horrified reaction
jokes.explanation: Good character beat showing Geoffrey's dread about Hilary in the kitchen.

jokes.text: Well, the only way you're gonna wear this one is if you wear that one over it.
jokes.setup: Ashley asking about which outfit to wear
jokes.punchline: Hilary saying she needs to wear both because one is too revealing
jokes.explanation: Standard sister dynamic joke about inappropriate clothing choices.

jokes.text: Bored! Yes. Yes, I'm bored. I'll do anything with anybody. Now, somebody ask me to do something.
jokes.setup: Everyone calling Will bored
jokes.punchline: Will's complete breakdown and desperate plea
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing Will hitting rock bottom of boredom. The desperation is palpable and funny.

jokes.text: I'm gonna go practice my swing. - I have better things to do.
jokes.setup: Will's desperate plea for someone to ask him to do something
jokes.punchline: Everyone immediately leaving
jokes.explanation: Good comedic beat - the family's complete dismissal of Will's plea.

jokes.text: You're getting a little freaky in your old age, huh, Uncle Phil?
jokes.setup: Uncle Phil mentioning a golf 'foursome'
jokes.punchline: Will misinterpreting it sexually
jokes.explanation: Standard double entendre joke - predictable but in character for Will.

jokes.text: You know, this is your last chance to make that a fifthsome.
jokes.setup: Continuing the foursome misunderstanding
jokes.punchline: Will suggesting he join the imaginary orgy
jokes.explanation: Weaker escalation of the foursome joke - pushes it too far without adding much.

jokes.text: He'll call.
jokes.setup: Uncle Phil leaving without responding to the fifthsome comment
jokes.punchline: Will's confident prediction despite the obvious rejection
jokes.explanation: Good tag line showing Will's delusional confidence after being ignored.

jokes.text: I'm afraid that ship has sailed.
jokes.setup: Hilary saying she'll look stupid on her show without learning to flambé
jokes.punchline: Geoffrey implying she already looks stupid
jokes.explanation: Exceptional Geoffrey burn - polite phrasing masking a devastating insult. Perfect character voice.

jokes.text: Geoffrey can't be bothered trying to teach a moron to cook. Right, Geoffrey?
jokes.setup: Hilary trying to get Geoffrey to exclude Will
jokes.punchline: She calls herself a moron while trying to insult Will
jokes.explanation: Classic Hilary self-own - her attempt to exclude Will backfires through her own stupidity.

jokes.text: I never knew bananas were so flammable.
jokes.setup: Watching the flambé demonstration
jokes.punchline: Hilary missing that it's the alcohol, not the bananas, that's flammable
jokes.explanation: Good Hilary stupidity moment - completely misunderstanding basic cooking science.

jokes.text: I can't flambé.
jokes.setup: Will attempting to flambé alone and presumably failing
jokes.punchline: His matter-of-fact admission of failure
jokes.explanation: Simple admission that sets up the disaster to come. The understatement is effective.

jokes.text: Voilà in reverse.
jokes.setup: Will trying to put out the fire he started
jokes.punchline: Describing his failure as the opposite of the French flourish
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay that perfectly captures the moment. The reversal concept is well-executed.

jokes.text: I don't care, you still have to pay a luxury tax.
jokes.setup: Ashley playing Monopoly while chaos erupts around her
jokes.punchline: Her complete focus on the board game despite the house burning
jokes.explanation: Brilliant visual/audio gag - the mundane Monopoly argument contrasted with the emergency creates perfect absurdity.

jokes.text: My house is on fire. I have to leave now.
jokes.setup: Carlton calling during the fire
jokes.punchline: Will's matter-of-fact explanation for hanging up
jokes.explanation: Perfect deadpan delivery of an emergency. The casual tone makes it funnier.

jokes.text: Auntie Em! Auntie Em!
jokes.setup: Will trapped in smoke and chaos
jokes.punchline: Wizard of Oz reference during his crisis
jokes.explanation: Standard pop culture reference - functional but not particularly clever.

jokes.text: Mama, no!
jokes.setup: Continuing his dramatic panic
jokes.punchline: Over-the-top emotional reaction to the kitchen fire
jokes.explanation: Standard over-dramatic reaction - fits Will's character but isn't exceptional.

jokes.text: Our work here is done.
jokes.setup: Survey of the completely destroyed kitchen
jokes.punchline: Treating destruction as successful completion of a mission
jokes.explanation: Good ironic comment that reframes disaster as achievement. Classic Will deflection.

jokes.text: Black folks ain't supposed to cook flambé. You stupid.
jokes.setup: Will explaining his kitchen disaster to himself
jokes.punchline: Making it about cultural appropriation of fancy cooking
jokes.explanation: Racial humor that fits Will's character and the show's style - finding cultural explanation for personal failure.

jokes.text: Hey, guys, sissy boy been cooking flambé.
jokes.setup: Will imagining what people will say
jokes.punchline: Projecting his own insecurities about fancy cooking
jokes.explanation: Shows Will's masculine insecurities but the execution is fairly standard.

jokes.text: And I poofed the kitchen down.
jokes.setup: Will explaining his flambé technique to Geoffrey
jokes.punchline: Using 'poofed' as both the cooking term and destruction
jokes.explanation: Decent wordplay that captures both the cooking technique and the disaster.

jokes.text: Farewell. - Cheerio.
jokes.setup: Geoffrey leaving Will to face the consequences
jokes.punchline: His increasingly formal British farewells
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of Geoffrey's formality as he abandons Will. The British terms add to his character.

jokes.text: Halston? Whoa, this is my stop.
jokes.setup: Will realizes who picked him up
jokes.punchline: His immediate desire to get out
jokes.explanation: Good follow-up to the coincidence - Will realizing he'd rather face the fire than Carlton.

jokes.text: You the one been out there swinging them golf clubs all day, you know what I mean?
jokes.setup: Judge asking about a smell
jokes.punchline: Will deflecting the smoke smell as golf sweat
jokes.explanation: Good improvisation by Will to cover the smoke smell - clever deflection.

jokes.text: Man, Uncle Phil got some books. Britannica, Webster's, Playboy.
jokes.setup: Will trying to distract the judge with the library
jokes.punchline: Including Playboy in the list of reference materials
jokes.explanation: Classic 'one of these things is not like the others' structure - effective but predictable.

jokes.text: Isn't that a pronoun that would include me?
jokes.setup: Will saying 'we dodged that bullet'
jokes.punchline: Geoffrey's grammatical correction rejecting inclusion
jokes.explanation: Exceptional Geoffrey moment - using grammar to distance himself from Will's disaster. Perfect character voice and structure.

jokes.text: Because murderers, they're felons, and felons, they can't be judges.
jokes.setup: Will explaining why Uncle Phil can't see the kitchen until after dinner
jokes.punchline: His convoluted logic about murder disqualifying judges
jokes.explanation: Great Will logic - twisted reasoning that almost makes sense. The legal terminology adds to the absurdity.

jokes.text: We should all just share the wealth, man.
jokes.setup: Will apparently having a political discussion with the judge
jokes.punchline: His Socialist rhetoric to a conservative judge
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Will's inappropriate political timing - trying to fill conversation while panicking.

jokes.text: Excellent judgment, you know what I'm saying? He got good judgment. Ain't that just like a judge? He a good judge.
jokes.setup: Will over-praising the judge's suggestion about the pitcher
jokes.punchline: His increasingly desperate and repetitive compliments
jokes.explanation: Good cringe moment showing Will's panic-induced over-praise, though the repetition goes on too long.

jokes.text: I don't know. Lack of a kitchen?
jokes.setup: Will asking why getting the pitcher is taking so long
jokes.punchline: Geoffrey's matter-of-fact response about the obvious obstacle
jokes.explanation: Perfect Geoffrey deadpan - pointing out the obvious absurdity of Will's question with impeccable timing and economy.

jokes.text: Ixnay on the Indian food. Judge can't take spicy.
jokes.setup: Will frantically coordinating the food cover-up
jokes.punchline: Using pig Latin while managing restaurant logistics
jokes.explanation: Good detail showing Will's frantic coordination - pig Latin adds to the conspiracy feel.

jokes.text: If I got to hose down some poultry to pull this off, that's what I'm gonna do.
jokes.setup: Will's determination to fix the dinner situation
jokes.punchline: Extreme measures described casually
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing Will's desperation - the casual tone about 'hosing down poultry' is funny.

jokes.text: Don't make me choose. Red or brown? Ooh...
jokes.setup: Will forcing Geoffrey to choose a sauce
jokes.punchline: Geoffrey's anguished difficulty with this trivial decision
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Geoffrey's proper standards being compromised - his pain over choosing between takeout sauces.

jokes.text: Do you ever wonder what makes your skin stay on?
jokes.setup: Dinner conversation
jokes.punchline: Carlton's completely random, disturbing question
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly absurd Carlton moment - completely out of nowhere and perfectly unsettling. The randomness is what makes it work.

jokes.text: Oh, sweet mama. Now, that's chicken.
jokes.setup: Judge eating what he thinks is Geoffrey's cooking
jokes.punchline: Praising takeout fried chicken as gourmet
jokes.explanation: Good irony - the judge's genuine appreciation for what he doesn't know is fast food.

jokes.text: Oh, thank God you're all right. / What are you doing here? / When Will told me the kitchen was on fire I didn't know what to expect.
jokes.setup: Carlton returns home concerned about the fire Will mentioned
jokes.punchline: Carlton's genuine concern contrasts with everyone's attempts to hide the damage
jokes.explanation: Good callback to the phone conversation - Carlton's genuine concern creates dramatic irony

jokes.text: You were able to prepare a magnificent meal like that in there?
jokes.setup: Judge seeing the destroyed kitchen
jokes.punchline: His amazement that good food came from a disaster zone
jokes.explanation: Good payoff to the kitchen reveal - the judge's increased respect based on wrong assumptions.

jokes.text: And this man will have you on the Supreme Court in no time.
jokes.setup: Judge praising Uncle Phil's 'special family'
jokes.punchline: Promising career advancement based on a completely false impression
jokes.explanation: Good ironic payoff - Uncle Phil gets what he wanted for completely wrong reasons.

jokes.text: You gonna let me go anytime soon? - No, Will. - That's cool.
jokes.setup: Uncle Phil presumably hugging Will tightly after the revelation
jokes.punchline: Will's resigned acceptance of his punishment
jokes.explanation: Good ending moment showing the family dynamic - Will accepting his fate with characteristic resignation.
